name: SonarQube MCP Server
what_it_does: Official SonarQube MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with SonarQube/SonarCloud code quality and security analysis — querying issues, security hotspots, quality gates, and code metrics.
use_cases: Querying SonarQube issues for code quality review agents
Checking quality gate status before deployment from CI/CD agents
Reading security hotspots for vulnerability triage agents
Analyzing code coverage and duplication metrics from agents
Automated code quality reporting in development workflows
not_for: Teams using Snyk, Veracode, or CodeClimate exclusively
Dynamic testing (DAST) — SonarQube is SAST only
best_when: An agent needs to query SonarQube/SonarCloud for code quality metrics, security issues, or quality gate status.
avoid_when: You're using a different SAST platform — use that provider's tools.

auth.notes: User tokens with project-level permissions. Global analysis tokens for CI. No fine-grained scopes — token access controlled by user permissions.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Community Edition: free self-hosted, limited features; SonarCloud: free for open source
pricing.notes: Community Edition is free self-hosted. SonarCloud free for public repos. Paid editions add advanced security rules, multi-branch analysis.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits configurable on self-hosted; SonarCloud has request limits

known_agent_gotchas: Project key (not name) required for most operations — use key for API calls
Self-hosted vs SonarCloud have different base URLs and feature sets
Quality Gate conditions are complex objects — agents must parse rule details
Issues can be in different states (open, resolved, etc.) — filter appropriately
Self-hosted TLS configuration is operator responsibility
API token has project-level access controlled by user permissions in Sonar
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced for SonarCloud; self-hosted requires manual TLS. User token auth with project permissions. SOC 2, ISO 27001, GDPR for cloud. Self-hosted security is operator responsibility.

security_score_components.security_notes: Cloud enforces HTTPS; self-hosted is operator's responsibility. User token with project permissions. SOC 2, ISO 27001. Code analysis data may be sensitive — secure accordingly.
